name: Corn Chowder-Daley
recipeIngredient: 6 cups Corn kernels, with cobs removed
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat a large, heavy saucepan over medium heat for about 1 minute.
2. Spray the pan twice with vegetable oil cooking spray.
3. Add onion and sauté until translucent, about 5 minutes.
4. Add 4 cups of corn and sauté until slightly softened, about 4-5 minutes.
5. Stir in 2 cups of chicken stock and cook until corn can be mashed easily with a fork, about 20 minutes.
6. Transfer contents to a blender and puree until smooth. Return to saucepan.
7. Add bell pepper, rosemary, thyme, black pepper, cayenne pepper, remaining chicken stock, and 2 cups of corn. Stir and cook for another 10 minutes until thick and creamy.
8. Garnish with chopped basil.
(Note: This recipe yields approximately 4 servings, each containing about 29 calories per serving.)
